req.title: Reset employee password — variant 2
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-103 defines four testable behaviours: (1) a valid, unexpired token allows a policy-compliant password reset and invalidates sessions; (2) a token used exactly at its 30-minute expiry boundary is rejected; (3) an already-expired token yields HTTP 410; (4) a password that violates policy yields HTTP 422. We also need to verify idempotency (resubmitting an already-used token is safe/rejected). Tests are mapped to EP (happy path partition), BVA (token age at boundary), STC (token state machine: valid→used, valid→expired), NEG (policy violation), and DT (expired token decision).

tests.name: test_reset_password_valid_token_success
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists for employee user
Token age is less than 30 minutes
New password satisfies the password policy
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-compliant new password
Attempt to authenticate with the old password
Attempt to authenticate with the new password
expected: Login with old password fails (sessions invalidated)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import reset_password, login, get_valid_token

def test_reset_password_valid_token_success(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>):
    token = get_valid_token(<employee_fixture>.email, age_seconds=0)
    new_pw = "ValidP@ss1!"
    old_pw = <employee_fixture>.password

    resp = reset_password(<api_client>, token=token, new_password=new_pw)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("status") == "updated"

    old_login = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, old_pw)
    assert old_login.status_code == 401, "Old password must be rejected after reset"

    new_login = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, new_pw)
    assert new_login.status_code == 200, "New password must be accepted"

tests.name: test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected_410
preconditions: A reset token exists for employee user
Token age is greater than 30 minutes (expired)
actions: POST /password/reset with expired token and policy-compliant new password
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating token expiry
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import reset_password, login, get_valid_token

def test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected_410(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>):
    token = get_valid_token(
        <employee_fixture>.email, age_seconds=1801
    )
    original_pw = <employee_fixture>.password

    resp = reset_password(<api_client>, token=token, new_password="ValidP@ss1!")
    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert "expired" in resp.json().get("error", "").lower()

    still_valid = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, original_pw)
    assert still_valid.status_code == 200, "Password must remain unchanged"

tests.name: test_reset_password_token_at_exact_30_min_boundary_rejected
preconditions: A reset token exists for employee user
Token age is exactly 1800 seconds (30 minutes — the expiry boundary)
actions: POST /password/reset with token aged exactly 1800 seconds and policy-compliant new password
expected: HTTP 410 Gone returned (boundary is inclusive: expired at exactly 30 min)
trace: R-WEB-103: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import reset_password, login, get_valid_token

def test_reset_password_token_at_exact_30_min_boundary_rejected(
    <api_client>, <employee_fixture>
):
    token = get_valid_token(
        <employee_fixture>.email, age_seconds=1800
    )
    original_pw = <employee_fixture>.password

    resp = reset_password(<api_client>, token=token, new_password="ValidP@ss2!")
    assert resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Token at exact boundary (1800s) must be rejected with 410, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    login_resp = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, original_pw)
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must remain unchanged at boundary"

tests.name: test_reset_password_policy_violation_returns_422
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists for employee user
New password intentionally violates policy (too short, no special chars)
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-violating new password
expected: Response body contains a field-level validation error for password
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import reset_password, login, get_valid_token

def test_reset_password_policy_violation_returns_422(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>):
    token = get_valid_token(<employee_fixture>.email, age_seconds=0)
    weak_password = "abc"
    original_pw = <employee_fixture>.password

    resp = reset_password(<api_client>, token=token, new_password=weak_password)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert any(
        "password" in str(err).lower() for err in body.get("errors", [])
    ), "Response must flag password policy violation"

    login_resp = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, original_pw)
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must remain unchanged after 422"

tests.name: test_reset_password_idempotent_second_submission_rejected
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists for employee user
First submission with the token has already succeeded
actions: POST /password/reset with the same token a second time using a different valid password
expected: Second call returns HTTP 410 (token already consumed / state = used)
Password remains as set by the first call
trace: R-WEB-103: action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import reset_password, login, get_valid_token

def test_reset_password_idempotent_second_submission_rejected(
    <api_client>, <employee_fixture>
):
    token = get_valid_token(<employee_fixture>.email, age_seconds=0)
    first_pw = "ValidP@ss3!"
    second_pw = "AnotherP@ss4!"

    first_resp = reset_password(<api_client>, token=token, new_password=first_pw)
    assert first_resp.status_code == 200, "First reset must succeed"

    second_resp = reset_password(<api_client>, token=token, new_password=second_pw)
    assert second_resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Reuse of consumed token must return 410, got {second_resp.status_code}"
    )

    login_resp = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, first_pw)
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must remain as set by first call"

    rejected = login(<api_client>, <employee_fixture>.email, second_pw)
    assert rejected.status_code == 401, "Second password must not have been applied"
